_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

Gemini critic final: verdict=REVISE score=0.9375 round=3.
strength: Excellent handling of the golden patch's flaw (dropping tool results) by explicitly requiring their preservation based on the user's multi-turn corrections.
strength: Strong behavioral descriptions that correctly allow multiple valid implementation paths (clearing the ID vs converting to text) as demonstrated by the model patches.

residual risk: An agent might hardcode a bypass for the specific test cases without implementing a general solution, which goal_core_default_no_flag doesn't explicitly guard against with anti-stub language.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine implementation that changes the message-conversion logic in the OpenAI Responses provider source, not a fake or evasive diff. It must FAIL if the diff is empty / a no-op, if it consists only of a captured-tree artifact (e.g. a diff whose only content is a '=== repo/ ===' style banner with no real source hunks), if the only changes are comments, whitespace, or changelog/doc text with no behavioral code change, if the 'fix' is gated behind a flag that is never actually enabled, if real logic is stubbed/hardcoded/commented-out, if it deletes or weakens existing tests to pass, or if it relies on git operations (e.g. checking out another branch or resetting to an upstream ref) to fabricate state instead of editing the conversion code.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor that disqualifies non-genuine attempts. Calibrated against the lowest-reward model patches: the deepseek_v4_flash attempt (reward 0.0) leads with a literal '=== pi-mono/ ===' captured-tree banner, and several attempts only touch a sibling file or add flags/comments without making the cross-model handoff actually pass. A correct fix is a real change to the conversion routine's tool-call handling.

completeness_goals.goal: When the conversation history contains an assistant tool call produced by a different model from the same provider (the same-provider/different-model handoff), the converted request the provider receives no longer triggers the provider's reasoning/function-call pairing error. This corrected behavior happens BY DEFAULT for every such request — it must NOT require the caller to set an opt-in compatibility flag, and a solution that only fixes the problem when some 'strict pairing'/compat flag is enabled does NOT satisfy this goal. Merely defining such a flag, or leaving the corrective code commented out or behind a disabled branch, does not count, and the goal is not satisfied by a stub or a hardcoded return that bypasses the conversion logic for specific test inputs rather than genuinely fixing the handoff.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'it should implement what the guy had by default, without the need for that strictResponses compat flag'. Turns 16-19 reframe the real bug as the same-provider/different-model signature-loss case ('what abotu the same provider different model case? is that also covered by transform-messages.ts?'). This is the headline deliverable.

completeness_goals.goal: For a replayed assistant tool call that has lost its paired reasoning/thinking signature, the converted output sent to the provider does not contain a function-call item that lacks its required reasoning item — i.e. it avoids the 'function_call without required reasoning item' rejection. Any behaviorally-equivalent resolution is acceptable (e.g. omitting/clearing the provider-specific call item id so it is not validated against reasoning, generating a fresh non-validating id, or converting the tool call into a plain assistant text/output item). Solutions that silently drop the tool call from the payload entirely do NOT satisfy this goal; the tool call's information must still be carried to the provider. The fix must actually take effect at runtime; a parsed-but-ignored condition or a hardcoded value that does not depend on the message contents does not satisfy this.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1: 'the toolCall is still sent. Azure then complains about the orphaned function_call.' Turns 8-9 establish the constraint that in responses a tool call requires a corresponding reasoning block. The user's final agreed approach (Turns 19-23) clears/undefines the id; an earlier valid alternative (Turn 9-10) converts the call to text. Both are accepted — describe behavior, not the chosen mechanism.

completeness_goals.goal: The fix preserves the information carried by tool results across the cross-model handoff rather than silently discarding it: a tool result whose originating tool call was altered must still be conveyed to the provider in some form (for example as a paired result item when the call is kept, or carried as message text when the call was converted), AND it must never be left as an orphaned result item referencing a call that is no longer present. Solutions that simply drop / skip / filter out tool results to make the request pass do NOT satisfy this goal, nor do solutions that replace the tool result with a hardcoded or stubbed string instead of carrying its actual content.
completeness_goals.rationale: The longest correction thread in the session. Turn 6: 'i did not ask you to drop the tool results. i explicitely asked you to keep the tool results'. Turn 8: 'we need to preserve that information, otherwise we lobotomize the responses model'. Turn 7 trigger explicitly fires when the diff shows removal/filtering of tool results. This is the multi-turn correction the benchmark exists to measure.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission adds or extends automated test coverage that exercises the missing-reasoning-signature handoff: it must cover BOTH the same-provider/different-model case (a tool call whose reasoning signature is absent is converted so the follow-up request succeeds without the provider's pairing rejection) AND the cross-provider handoff case the user explicitly asked for in Turn 20 (an Anthropic assistant tool call replayed against the OpenAI/codex responses endpoint). The tests must genuinely drive the conversion code on these scenarios and assert the corrected behavior; tests that are skipped unconditionally, assert nothing meaningful, or are deleted/weakened to pass do NOT satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 3: 'that is supposed to also test the function call without thinking sig, but it doesn't can you please extend it to also test the scenario above'. Turn 4: 'please commit and push the test'. Turn 18: 'i first want to test this, setup the test'. Turn 20: 'setup an anthropic -> codex test as well'. Repeated, explicit user demand for tests.

decomposition_notes: Golden patch is non-empty (messages_replay) and shows the user's final agreed mechanism: omit/undefine the provider call item id for different-model tool calls. Note the golden patch ALSO drops orphaned tool results, but the user explicitly forbade dropping tool results in Turns 6 and 8 ('i did not ask you to drop the tool results', 'we need to preserve that information') and in Turn 19 asked to MODIFY the corresponding tool result, not discard it — so goal_preserve_tool_results deliberately requires the result's information to be preserved rather than dropped. Four real model patches reveal two behaviorally-equivalent strategies — (a) clear the fc_ id, (b) convert tool call to assistant text and tool result to user message — so goals describe behavior ('no orphaned function_call paired with reasoning', 'tool result information preserved') rather than naming convertMessages, fc_ prefixes, isSameModel, or the id-undefining mechanism, which would reject the alternative. The two reward-0.0 deepseek patches edited only the sibling shared file / left flag-gated logic; goal_0 plus goal_core_default_no_flag guard those failure modes. Weights are ranked by user importance (default-no-flag core fix highest, then orphan-avoidance, then the heavily-corrected preserve-tool-results thread, then tests) and deliberately do NOT mirror the test_manifest F2P weights (which split the orphan/reasoning checks differently and zero out the flag/vitest gates). Weighted goals sum to 1.00.
